title: High Density Resting State EEG of Phantom Limb Pain and Controls
description: This dataset contains high-density resting-state EEG recordings (64 or 128 channels) from three subject groups: amputees with phantom limb pain, amputees without phantom limb pain, and pain-free intact controls. Approximately 7-minute recordings were acquired with eyes open or closed conditions. The data is organized according to BIDS standards and includes session-level pain questionnaire phenotype data, facilitating accessible reuse for neurophysiological investigations of phantom limb pain mechanisms.
methods_description: High-density EEG data (64 or 128 channels) were collected during resting-state conditions with eyes open or closed, as indicated by task designation. Recordings were approximately 7 minutes in duration.
